title: Nyla's Bouncy Ball Experiment
synopsis: When Nyla's favorite bouncy ball starts acting weird, she becomes a scientist to figure out what's wrong. With help from her friends, she discovers the silly science behind bouncy balls and learns that even failed experiments can lead to fun discoveries.

pages.text: Nyla loved her purple bouncy ball. She bounced it every day after school. Bounce, bounce, bounce! It went up high and came down fast. But today something was wrong. The ball only bounced a little bit. Then it just rolled away. 'That's weird,' said Nyla.

illustration.description: A young Black girl with curly hair in two puffs, wearing a bright yellow t-shirt and blue jeans, looking puzzled as she holds a purple bouncy ball. Her large expressive eyes show confusion. The setting is a sunny backyard with green grass and colorful flowers.
illustration.alt: Nyla looking confused while holding her purple bouncy ball in the backyard

pages.text: Nyla picked up the ball. She bounced it again. Boing... plop. It barely came back to her hand. 'Mom!' she called. 'My ball is broken!' Mom came outside. She had paint on her apron and smiled at Nyla. 'What's wrong with it?' she asked.

illustration.description: Nyla's mom, a Black woman with natural hair in a headwrap, wearing a colorful paint-splattered apron, kneeling down next to Nyla in the backyard. Both characters have warm expressions, with Nyla showing her ball to her mom. Bright afternoon lighting with vibrant greens and blues.
illustration.alt: Nyla showing her bouncy ball to her mom who is kneeling beside her

pages.text: 'Look!' Nyla bounced the ball. It went up just a tiny bit. Mom laughed. 'Hmm, that is strange. Maybe you need to be a scientist to figure this out.' 'A scientist?' asked Nyla. Her eyes got big and excited. 'Yes! Scientists ask questions and find answers,' said Mom.

illustration.description: Nyla with huge excited eyes and a big smile, hands clasped together in delight, while her mom points upward with one finger as if having an idea. The purple ball sits on the ground between them. Warm golden sunlight creates a cheerful mood with rich saturated colors.
illustration.alt: Nyla looking excited about becoming a scientist while mom explains

pages.text: The next day, Nyla wore her lab coat. It was really Dad's old white shirt, but it made her feel like a real scientist. She got a notebook and wrote 'BOUNCY BALL MYSTERY' at the top. Her friend Marcus came over. 'What are you doing?' he asked.

illustration.description: Nyla wearing an oversized white button-up shirt like a lab coat, holding a colorful notebook with a big smile. Marcus, a Latino boy with short dark hair wearing a red striped shirt, stands nearby looking curious. They're in Nyla's bedroom with toys and books visible in the background.
illustration.alt: Nyla in her makeshift lab coat holding a notebook, with her friend Marcus looking curious

pages.text: 'I'm being a scientist!' said Nyla. 'My ball won't bounce right.' Marcus picked up the purple ball. 'It feels... squishy,' he said. 'And kind of cold.' Nyla wrote in her notebook: 'Ball is squishy and cold.' 'Good observation!' she said, sounding very scientific.

illustration.description: Marcus squeezing the purple ball with a thoughtful expression while Nyla writes in her notebook with intense concentration. Her tongue sticks out slightly as she writes. The scene is in bright daylight with cheerful colors, showing their focused detective work.
illustration.alt: Marcus examining the squishy ball while Nyla writes observations in her notebook

pages.text: Just then, Nyla's other friend Zara knocked on the door. Zara had red hair and freckles. She loved science too. 'What's happening?' she asked. 'We're solving a mystery!' said Nyla. She showed Zara the weird bouncy ball. 'Interesting,' said Zara, pushing up her pretend glasses.

illustration.description: Zara, a girl with curly red hair, freckles, and wearing purple overalls, making an exaggerated thinking pose with her finger on her chin. Nyla and Marcus watch her with anticipation. The room is filled with warm, inviting colors and has a playful scientific atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Zara making a thoughtful scientific pose while examining the mystery with Nyla and Marcus

pages.text: The three friends went outside. 'Let's test other balls!' said Nyla. Marcus found a tennis ball. Zara brought a rubber ball from her backpack. They lined up all the balls. 'Now we bounce them and see what happens,' said Nyla importantly.

illustration.description: Three children standing in a line in the backyard, each holding different colored balls. Nyla holds her purple ball, Marcus has a yellow tennis ball, and Zara has a bright red rubber ball. They look determined and excited, with a clear blue sky and green grass background.
illustration.alt: Nyla, Marcus, and Zara lined up holding different balls ready for their experiment

pages.text: Marcus bounced his tennis ball first. BOING! It went high up. 'Normal bounce!' wrote Nyla. Zara bounced her rubber ball. BOUNCE! It went even higher. 'Super bounce!' wrote Nyla. Then Nyla bounced her ball. Boing... plop. 'Weird bounce,' she wrote sadly.

illustration.description: Action scene showing the three different balls at various heights - the tennis ball mid-bounce, the rubber ball bouncing very high, and Nyla's purple ball barely leaving the ground. The children's expressions show excitement for the working balls and concern for Nyla's ball. Dynamic motion lines emphasize the bouncing.
illustration.alt: Three balls bouncing at different heights showing the experiment results

pages.text: 'Wait!' said Zara. 'Where did you keep your ball?' Nyla thought. 'In the basement,' she said. 'It's cold down there.' Zara's eyes got big. 'That's it! Cold makes rubber less bouncy!' Marcus clapped. 'You solved it!' But Nyla looked confused. 'How do we fix it?'

illustration.description: Zara with a lightbulb moment expression, eyes wide with discovery, pointing one finger up excitedly. Marcus is clapping with joy, and Nyla looks puzzled but hopeful. The scene has bright, celebratory colors with dynamic poses showing the eureka moment.
illustration.alt: Zara having a eureka moment while Marcus celebrates and Nyla looks puzzled about the solution

pages.text: 'We need to warm up the ball!' said Zara. Nyla's mom came outside with lemonade. 'How's your experiment going?' she asked. 'We found the problem!' said Nyla. 'The ball was too cold!' Mom smiled. 'What's your solution?' The kids looked at each other.

illustration.description: Mom carrying a tray with a pitcher of yellow lemonade and glasses, smiling warmly at the three children who are gathered around looking proud of their discovery. The scene is bathed in warm afternoon sunlight with rich, cheerful colors throughout the backyard setting.
illustration.alt: Mom bringing lemonade to the three young scientists who are sharing their discovery

pages.text: 'Let's rub the ball!' said Marcus. They took turns rubbing the purple ball with their hands. 'It's getting warmer!' said Nyla. After five minutes of rubbing, the ball felt normal again. 'Time for the test!' announced Zara dramatically.

illustration.description: The three children sitting in a circle on the grass, taking turns warming the purple ball by rubbing it between their hands. Their faces show concentration and hope. The warm sunlight creates a cozy feeling, and their expressions are focused and determined.
illustration.alt: Three children sitting in a circle warming up the purple ball by rubbing it

pages.text: Nyla stood up. She held her breath. She bounced the ball. BOING! It shot up high and came down fast. 'It works!' she shouted. All three friends cheered. Mom clapped from the porch. 'Excellent scientific work!' she called. Nyla felt very proud.

illustration.description: Nyla jumping with joy, arms raised in celebration, while the purple ball bounces high in the air. Marcus and Zara are cheering with big smiles. Mom waves from the porch in the background. The scene is full of vibrant colors and captures pure joy and triumph.
illustration.alt: Nyla celebrating with arms raised as her ball bounces high, with friends cheering

pages.text: The friends played with all the balls for the rest of the day. 'We're really good scientists!' said Marcus. 'We asked questions, tested things, and found the answer!' said Zara. Nyla bounced her ball happily. 'And now I know to keep my ball somewhere warm!'

illustration.description: The three friends running and playing in the backyard with various balls bouncing around them. They all look joyful and energetic. The late afternoon sun creates golden lighting, and colorful balls are captured mid-bounce throughout the scene, creating a dynamic, playful atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Three friends happily playing with bouncing balls in the golden afternoon light

pages.text: That night, Nyla put her ball in her warm bedroom instead of the cold basement. She wrote in her science notebook: 'Cold makes balls less bouncy. Warm makes balls bouncy again. Science is fun!' Then she smiled and went to sleep, dreaming of her next experiment.

illustration.description: Nyla in her cozy bedroom at nighttime, writing in her notebook by lamplight. The purple ball sits on her nightstand. Through the window, stars twinkle in a deep blue sky. The scene has warm yellow lighting from the lamp, creating a peaceful, satisfied mood as she completes her scientific journal.
illustration.alt: Nyla writing in her science notebook in her bedroom at night with her ball safely nearby

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Why wasn't Nyla's ball bouncing well?
questions.explanation: Zara discovered that Nyla kept her ball in the cold basement, and cold temperatures make rubber less bouncy!

questions.question: What did the friends do to fix the ball?
choices: Rubbed it to warm it up
questions.explanation: The friends rubbed the ball with their hands for five minutes to warm it up, which made it bouncy again!

questions.question: Who figured out what was wrong with the ball?
questions.explanation: Zara asked where Nyla kept the ball and realized that the cold basement was making it less bouncy!

questions.question: What did Nyla wear to feel like a scientist?
choices: A lab coat from the store
questions.explanation: Nyla wore her dad's old white shirt as a makeshift lab coat to feel like a real scientist!

questions.question: Where did Nyla decide to keep her ball at the end?
questions.explanation: Nyla learned from her experiment and decided to keep her ball in her warm bedroom instead of the cold basement!

pages.title: Complete the Science Steps
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to show how Nyla solved her bouncy ball mystery!
pages.text: First, Nyla noticed her ball wouldn't ___1___. Then she became a ___2___ to find out why. Her friends helped her ___3___ different balls. Zara discovered the ball was too ___4___. Finally, they ___5___ up the ball to fix it!
